Round: # Log EVERY cash movement as one row. Each team's statement Cash line = its Start Cash + the sum of these rows (SUMIF on column B).
# Amount is SIGNED: income / collect = positive (e.g. 500); spending / pay = negative (e.g. -200).
# Type vocabulary: Payday, Bill, Tax, Card, Deal, AssetBuy, Skill, Trade, Other.
#   Payday = collect net cashflow. Bill / Tax = fixed charges. Card = a Life Happens / Campus Life / Temptation draw.
#   Deal = buy/sell an Opportunity asset. AssetBuy = buy an ownable board asset. Skill = a Skill-Up purchase. Trade = stock buy/sell. Other = anything else (e.g. a term-deposit lock).

# PAYDAY amount is NOT fixed: read the team's current 'Monthly Cash Flow' off their Financial Statement and log THAT as the + Payday row. (It already nets their recurring rent/subs/debt - so never log those recurring expenses as separate rows; only one-off bills, taxes, and card draws get their own row.)

# AssetBuy / Deal / Skill: log the cash row here, AND add the matching row to the Statement Items tab. An Asset/Deal -> Asset row (lifts Passive Income, helps escape). A Skill -> just an Income row (lifts active Income / Cash Flow only).

# BALANCE-GROWTH cards (e.g. 'student-loan balance grows $1000', 'card balance grows $500') are NOT cash - do NOT log them here. Instead just raise that liability's BALANCE on Statement Items; its monthly payment recalculates itself (=balance x rate). Likewise a one-off 'lose $X income this payday' card IS cash - log it here as a negative row.
# EXAMPLES: the Round-1 rows above are illustrative - DELETE them before the live game so they don't count in Cash totals.
# These guide rows carry no team name in column B, so SUMIF ignores them - leaving them in place is harmless.
